response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a person who spends much of their day on social media, I've seen plenty of talk about COVID vaccines. It's been quite the whirlwind since these shots came out! But you know what? With my busy schedule as a delivery driver in Rome, I need to stay sharp and not get dragged into pointless arguments online because that can stir up some anxiety for me – even though I'm generally okay mentally, those heated debates about vaccines do make me feel uneasy. My doctor is amazing; he's an expert who always takes time with patients like us regular folk without a high-level degree but lots of life experience.

I remember being raised by parents: one has a PhD in biology – oh wait no, my mom does, dad not listed probably because it's vocational too! But they taught me to be open-minded even if I'm low on openness score myself. Now with all this vaccine chatter online, some folks get really emotional saying vaccines are dangerous or that the government is forcing everyone into them for nefarious reasons.

I can handle my own opinions thanks to my high extraversion level – I love talking about things like this at work sometimes when customers ask questions! But managing discussions means being respectful even if people don't agree. That's why I also enjoy playing board games which encourages good-natured debate without shouting matches.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
As a delivery driver in Rome with daily social media use, I encounter many vaccine discussions online.

My vocational diploma and low openness score mean I rely on trusted sources like my doctor for accurate information rather than complex debates myself.

Being married with good mental health helps me stay calm during arguments but my moderate neuroticism makes those heated discussions somewhat uncomfortable.
